game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Players drag and drop various geometric blocks onto a 9x9 grid, aiming to fill entire rows or columns to clear them and earn points, with the game ending when no more blocks can be placed from the current set of three available. Blocks cannot be rotated, and strategic placement is key to maximizing space and achieving high scores, with combo bonuses awarded for clearing multiple lines simultaneously or consecutively.
game_info.description: Block Blast is a strategic puzzle game where you place different geometric blocks on a 9x9 grid. Your goal is to fill entire rows or columns to clear them and earn points. You have three blocks available at a time - use arrow keys to select a block, then position and place it on the grid. The game ends when you can't place any of the available blocks. Plan your moves carefully to maximize your score with combo bonuses for clearing multiple lines at once!
game_info.controls:
LEFT ARROW: Move selected block or cursor left
RIGHT ARROW: Move selected block or cursor right
UP ARROW: Move selected block or cursor up
DOWN ARROW: Move selected block or cursor down
SPACE: Place the selected block on the grid
Z: Cycle through available blocks
ENTER: Start the game from the start screen
ESC: Pause/unpause the game
R: Restart the game (works on game over screen too)
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Tests basic gameplay functionality by placing blocks randomly on the grid to ensure the game loads without errors and responds to inputs correctly.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Uses sticky keys to select blocks and place them in available spaces on the grid, cycling through blocks when needed.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game should run without errors, blocks should be placed on the grid, and lines should be cleared when completed.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>
<TEST_2>
<test_description>Tests the win condition by strategically placing blocks to achieve a high score and clear multiple lines.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Places blocks to prioritize clearing rows and columns, creating combos whenever possible to maximize score.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Player should reach a high score with multiple line clears and combos, demonstrating the game can be won.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>
<TEST_3>
<test_description>Tests the block selection mechanism to ensure players can cycle through available blocks properly.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Frequently cycles through the available blocks using the Z key while playing to test block selection functionality.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Player should be able to successfully cycle through all available blocks and place them on the grid.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>
<TEST_4>
<test_description>Tests the line clearing mechanism to ensure rows and columns are properly cleared when filled.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Focuses on filling specific rows and columns completely to trigger line clears.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Completed rows and columns should disappear from the grid, and the score should increase accordingly.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>
<TEST_5>
<test_description>Tests the game over condition by deliberately filling the grid in a way that makes placing new blocks impossible.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Places blocks in a pattern that eventually makes it impossible to place any of the available blocks.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game should transition to the GAME_OVER_LOSE phase when no more blocks can be placed.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
